title: Old Translated Newtype Articles about Eva
display_title: Old Translated Newtype Articles about Eva
wikitext:
[]: Old Translated Newtype Articles about Eva             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Oct 12, 2010 4:45 am Here there are many. A lot of what's said is speculation and memory-refreshment... much like Newtype's articles on NME today. To me, the most interesting discovery is a collection of statements made by Anno while the show was still in production. Anno seemed in high spirits near the beginning of the show's production when Newtype visited Gainax in January, 1995: Hideaki Anno wrote: "It isn't completed yet, but in episodes one and two my recent 'feelings' should be faithfully reflected. When I realized this I thought 'Ah, well done.' " "I think this will become a greater cult film than 'Nadia', because there will probably not be another work with this 'feeling'." "How could I think of doing an old-fashioned robot anime? One reason is that we thought it would be good to put on TV a robot anime that is not sponsored by a toy company." "Robot anime has been stuck in a pattern, and we wanted to break out of it." "For example, I wonder if a person over the age of twenty who likes robot anime is really happy. He could find greater happiness elsewhere. Regretably, I have my doubts about his happiness."

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Oct 12, 2010 4:54 am Well, now I have source for the classic quote about "I wonder if anyone who's over 20 and really loves giant robots is truly happy" Thanks NAW, you're just awesome with these things. Reading now >

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Tue Oct 12, 2010 6:18 am Ayanami Rei Voice by: Hayashibara Megumi Pilot of Evangelion device #0. Reticent, rarely showing her emotions, a nihilist

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Tue Oct 12, 2010 7:20 am LeoXiao wrote: Rei's a nihilist?

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Tue Oct 12, 2010 7:36 am lol I dunno, she seemed pretty content to serve Gendo for most of the time.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Tue Oct 12, 2010 9:18 am +10 and a bagful of Mrs. Fields chocolate chip cookies for this, Nav. Most excellent find!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Oct 12, 2010 1:20 pm Chrad wrote: She believes in nothing, LeoXiao. Nothing. that stuck out to me too

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 12, 2010 4:20 pm These look somewhat familiar. Either way, sources for things are always nice. Thanks, Naw-kun!

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Mon Jan 24, 2011 1:40 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: These look somewhat familiar. Either way, sources for things are always nice.

[]: Old Translated Newtype Articles about Eva             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Oct 12, 2010 4:45 am Here there are many. A lot of what's said is speculation and memory-refreshment... much like Newtype's articles on NME today. To me, the most interesting discovery is a collection of statements made by Anno while the show was still in production. Anno seemed in high spirits near the beginning of the show's production when Newtype visited Gainax in January, 1995: Hideaki Anno wrote: "It isn't completed yet, but in episodes one and two my recent 'feelings' should be faithfully reflected. When I realized this I thought 'Ah, well done.' " "I think this will become a greater cult film than 'Nadia', because there will probably not be another work with this 'feeling'." "How could I think of doing an old-fashioned robot anime? One reason is that we thought it would be good to put on TV a robot anime that is not sponsored by a toy company." "Robot anime has been stuck in a pattern, and we wanted to break out of it." "For example, I wonder if a person over the age of twenty who likes robot anime is really happy. He could find greater happiness elsewhere. Regretably, I have my doubts about his happiness."
